Guide

# make-error

(opens new window) (opens new window) (opens new window) (opens new window)

Make your own error types!

# Features

  • Compatible Node & browsers
  • instanceof support
  • error.name & error.stack support
  • compatible with CSP (opens new window) (i.e. no eval())

# Installation

# Node & Browserify (opens new window)/Webpack (opens new window)

Installation of the npm package (opens new window):

> npm install --save make-error

Then require the package:

var makeError = require("make-error");

# Browser

You can directly use the build provided at unpkg.com (opens new window):

<script src="https://unpkg.com/make-error@1/dist/make-error.js"></script>

# Usage

# Basic named error

var CustomError = makeError("CustomError");

// Parameters are forwarded to the super class (here Error).
throw new CustomError("a message");

# Advanced error class

function CustomError(customValue) {
  CustomError.super.call(this, "custom error message");

  this.customValue = customValue;
}
makeError(CustomError);

// Feel free to extend the prototype.
CustomError.prototype.myMethod = function CustomError$myMethod() {
  console.log("CustomError.myMethod (%s, %s)", this.code, this.message);
};

//-----

try {
  throw new CustomError(42);
} catch (error) {
  error.myMethod();
}

# Specialized error

var SpecializedError = makeError("SpecializedError", CustomError);

throw new SpecializedError(42);

# Inheritance

Best for ES2015+.

import { BaseError } from "make-error";

class CustomError extends BaseError {
  constructor() {
    super("custom error message");
  }
}

# Related

  • make-error-cause (opens new window): Make your own error types, with a cause!

# Contributions

Contributions are very welcomed, either on the documentation or on the code.

You may:

  • report any issue (opens new window) you've encountered;
  • fork and create a pull request.
